user_query:
I’ve been a pretty bookish kid, but since I started taking short exercise breaks between study sessions, my focus and stress levels got way better. To keep this habit consistent, I want a quick, repeatable check of my laptop’s health right after a workout so I don’t get derailed by a sluggish system.

Please do a small, local system status inspection and produce two outputs, then update my routine log:

Inputs you must use:
- templates/status_template.md (layout guidance for the report)
- logs/study_routine_log.md (edit this file in-place)
- tests/validate_outputs.py (run this to validate your results)

Required outputs (create these paths):
1) output/system_snapshot.json
   - Populate with live values from this machine (do not hardcode). Include at least these fields with the exact keys and types:
     - timestamp (ISO 8601 string, e.g., 2026-04-15T13:45:00)
     - os_name (string)
     - kernel (string)
     - hostname (string)
     - cpu_model (string)
     - logical_cores (integer ≥ 1)
     - mem_total_mb (integer > 0)
     - mem_available_mb (integer ≥ 0 and ≤ mem_total_mb)
     - load_avg_1m, load_avg_5m, load_avg_15m (floats ≥ 0)
     - fs_path (string set to ".")
     - fs_total_gb (float > 0)
     - fs_used_gb (float ≥ 0)
     - fs_used_percent (float between 0 and 100)
     - top_processes (list of exactly 3 items; each item has pid (int), command (string), rss_mb (float ≥ 0))
   - Gather data using standard, non-privileged methods only (e.g., /proc, uname, df, ps). Keep it self-contained—no internet, no root.

2) output/system_status_report.md
   - Compose a concise Markdown status report following the structure suggested by templates/status_template.md. Include the same core values from output/system_snapshot.json so the two files agree on: timestamp, cpu_model, logical_cores, mem_total_mb, and fs_used_percent.
   - Include a short "Notes on Focus" section (2–3 sentences) that ties the current system state to my study+exercise rhythm (e.g., whether load/memory look suitable after a quick workout).

Update the existing log:
- Edit logs/study_routine_log.md in-place. Replace ONLY the content inside the markers:
  <!-- BEGIN LAST CHECK --> ... <!-- END LAST CHECK -->
  with a fresh summary that includes:
  - Date: YYYY-MM-DD (derived from the JSON timestamp)
  - Report: output/system_status_report.md
  - Snapshot: output/system_snapshot.json
  - A one-sentence status note (e.g., "Load looks steady for studying after a stretch break.")
  Keep all other sections of the log intact.

Validation (must run before delivering):
- Run: python tests/validate_outputs.py output/system_snapshot.json output/system_status_report.md logs/study_routine_log.md
- Only deliver once the validator exits successfully. If it fails, adjust the outputs to comply.

Assume a typical Linux-like environment. Please keep the workflow offline and reproducible. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
